entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
The recent discourse among programmers highlights a critical analysis of Bitcoin's transaction mechanisms and the potential implications of incorporating advanced support features such as covenant support and Mimblewimble Extension Blocks (MWEB). The primary concern revolves around balancing the simplicity of Bitcoin's functionality with its principle of finite supply. The introduction of enhanced transaction features could potentially lead to increased transaction fees. This, in turn, might provide a sustainable revenue model for Bitcoin, relying solely on fees without necessitating further expansion or dilution of its limited supply.
The implementation of these advanced features, including on-chain mixing protocols and vaults, is posited as a solution that could not only enhance Bitcoin's utility but also secure higher fees from users. By doing so, it could alleviate some of the financial pressures associated with maintaining the network as it scales. This approach suggests a strategic shift from merely expanding block demand to improving transactional capabilities and services within the existing framework, which could ultimately support Bitcoin's longevity and stability solely through user fees.
